assembly_instructions: Custom-fit 3D-printed myoelectric below-elbow prosthesis. A cylindrical printed Nylon-12 socket is scanned and printed to match the wearer's residual limb, with a soft rim at the open elbow end. Two skin-contact EMG sensors live on the inside wall of the socket. A 180° manual-rotation wrist ring connects the socket to the printed palm / hand frame. Five independently driven fingers (thumb opposed + index, middle, ring, pinky) extend forward, each shown as three visible segments (proximal, middle, distal). Each finger's small DC gearmotor sits at its root inside the palm. A control board and battery pack are shown above the socket for inspectability (physically they live inside the socket). A magnetic 'hero cover' outer plate and its latch are shown above, representing the swappable cosmetic skin.

info.japanese_name: Open Bionics Hero Arm（筋電義手）
info.english_name: Open Bionics Hero Arm — Myoelectric Prosthetic Arm
info.summary: 3D-printed, direct-print, myoelectric below-elbow prosthesis from Open Bionics. EMG sensors on the residual limb translate muscle signals into six pre-programmed grips.
info.description: The Hero Arm is a 3D-printed myoelectric below-elbow prosthesis developed by Open Bionics (Bristol, UK). It replaces the hand and forearm of a transradial amputee with a custom-fit socket that is 3D-scanned from the residual limb, ensuring a personalized, comfortable fit. Two skin-contact EMG sensors on the inner wall of the socket pick up muscle signals from the remaining forearm; an embedded STM32-based control board classifies those signals in real time and drives five independent finger motors to execute one of several pre-programmed grip patterns (fist, tripod, pinch, hook, key-grip, point). A 180°-rotation wrist ring lets the user pronate/supinate the hand for natural posing. The powered parts sit under a magnetically-attached external cover that snaps off for battery swaps and comes in multiple colors and themed 'hero covers' (Iron Man, Star Wars, Frozen, etc.). The entire structural shell is printed in laser-sintered Nylon 12 for toughness, and Open Bionics publishes portions of the design to support clinical fitting and aftermarket customization.
info.operator: Fitted by partner clinics; used worldwide by transradial amputees including pediatric users
info.contract_value: ≈£10k (UK NHS reference pricing; varies by fit)
info.status: In production (covered by the NHS in the UK, various insurers elsewhere)

facts.label: Type
facts.value: Below-elbow myoelectric prosthesis, five independently motored fingers

facts.label: Control
facts.value: Dual skin-contact EMG sensors → STM32-based classifier → 6 grip programs

facts.label: Construction
facts.value: SLS laser-sintered Nylon-12 shell; off-the-shelf motors, battery, STM32

facts.label: Socket
facts.value: Fully custom — 3D-scanned from the residual limb

facts.label: Covers
facts.value: Magnetic swap-out hero covers (many finishes and themed skins)

facts.label: License
facts.value: Commercial product; design elements open for clinical integration

sources.title: Open Bionics — Hero Arm product page

parts.name: Forearm Socket (Nylon-12)
parts.role: Custom-fit cylindrical socket — 3D-scanned from the residual limb for a personalized, comfortable fit. Houses the control board, battery, and EMG sensors.

parts.name: Socket Rim (elbow end)
parts.role: Soft rubber rim at the open elbow end of the socket — comfort pad against the residual limb.

parts.name: Wrist Rotation Ring (180°)
parts.role: Manual 180°-rotation ring between the socket and the hand — for pronation/supination.

parts.name: Palm / Hand Frame
parts.role: Printed Nylon-12 palm frame. Roots all five fingers and houses the per-finger drive motors.

parts.name: Finger Motor — Thumb
parts.role: Small DC gearmotor at the thumb root — one of five independent finger motors.

parts.name: EMG Sensor (Port)
parts.role: Skin-contact surface-EMG electrode on the interior wall of the socket — picks up flexor muscle signals.

parts.name: EMG Sensor (Starboard)
parts.role: Second EMG electrode on the opposite interior wall — extensor muscle signals.

parts.name: STM32 Control Board (shown)
parts.role: STM32-based classifier + 5-channel motor driver. Physically inside the socket; shown above for inspectability.

parts.name: Battery Pack (shown)
parts.role: Li-ion battery pack — swapped through the external cover. Shown above for inspectability.

parts.name: External 'Hero' Cover
parts.role: Magnetically-attached outer cover — available in themed 'hero' finishes. Snaps off for battery swaps.

parts.name: Cover Magnetic Latch
parts.role: Representative magnetic latch pin — one of several that hold the hero cover on.
